node.statement: (Finiteness of d_F via Weis-Shirokov) By Weis-Shirokov Corollary 5 (external ref, verified): if K is a convex set and C is defined by ℓ generalized affine constraints, and x is extreme in C (so F_C(x) = {x} has dimension 0), then dim(F_K(x)) ∈ {0, 1, ..., ℓ}. Hence d_F is automatically finite for extreme points under finitely many constraints, regardless of dim(V). No operator-algebraic assumptions needed.
